Amaranthus blitum, also known as Amarenus patens, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family, commonly referred to as a flowering or edible amaranth. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Amaranthus blitum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and supplements, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
